content.text: Manager: Hi Jessica. What can I do for you?
Jessica: Hi Mary.I'd really like to discussthe new Paris project.Could we talk aboutthe distribution of tasks?
Manager: Sure! What's on your mind?
Jessica: Well, I really don't feel I have enough space for all the tasks assigned to me at the moment. I do realize we're short-staffed, but I'm really overworked.
Manager: I'm sorry to hear that.
Jessica: Thanks.I would like to make a suggestion that will benefit both sides.

Jessica:If youlet me not do the research, the sourcing of subcontractors and the paperwork connected with hiring them,I'llhave more time to do the graphics design work. I feel that's my strength, and I could really do a lot for the team here.
Manager: I see.I can go along with that,but on condition that you come up with a plan on how to distribute the tasks within the team.
Jessica:I think we’ve got a deal.Listen, there's one more thing. I'd like to ask if I could take the next 2 weeks off. I know this is short notice, but I really could do with a vacation now.
Manager:I’m afraid that’s out of the question.Like you said yourself, we're short-staffed at the moment and I just can't agree to that. Sorry.
Jessica: I see. Thanks anyway for helping me out with the Paris project. I really appreciate that.

content.text: Read a text about taxes. Do you know all the vocabularyin bold?

Taxes are an essential aspect of any economy, as they provide the government with the necessary funds tosubsidizepublic services and infrastructure. For individuals,annual income taxis calculated based on theirgross income, which is the total amount earned before any deductions orclaimsare made. From the gross income, deductions and claims can be made, resulting in thenet income, which is the amount that is actually taxed. For businesses, the amount of tax owed is typically calculated based on theirturnoveror profits. Some organizations or individuals may betax-exempt, meaning they are not required to make taxsubmissionsor pay income tax. Understanding the basics of taxation is important for anyone who wants to manage their finances effectively.
Read the definitions below. Which phrasesin bolddo they refer to?

items: The act of submitting documents or forms to an authority, such as tax returns to the government.
The total amount of income earned before any deductions or expenses are taken into account.
Excluded from paying certain taxes, such as income tax, due to specific reasons or circumstances.
A tax on the income earned by individuals or businesses, calculated as a percentage of their earnings.
The income remaining after all deductions and expenses have been subtracted from gross income.

content.text: The third conditional is a way to talk about things that did not happen in the past. In business, people use it to discuss missed opportunities or mistakes. For example, if a manager says, "If we had invested in technology last year, we would have increased profits," they mean that the company did not invest, and as a result, they did not achieve higher profits. Another example could be, "If we had launched the product earlier, we might have captured more of the market." Here, the company missed the chance because the product was launched later than ideal.

content.text: A mixed conditional is when we use two different time references in one conditional sentence. An example is: "If we had negotiated a better contract, we would have a lot of money now." Here, the first part talks about the past (we didn't negotiate the contract better), and the second part talks about the present result (we don't have a lot of money now). This shows how a past action affects the present situation.

content.text: Read the following opinions. Do you agree or disagree with each one of them? Why?

items: I think buying luxurious products is not necessary. Spending lots of money on expensive things is wasteful. It could be better to save or use money for more important needs, like education or helping others. Luxurious items might bring happiness, but only for a short time.

Explaining features and benefits helps people understand why something is useful or important. Features are the details about what something has or can do. Benefits show how these features help make life easier or better. By knowing both, people can make smarter choices about what they need or want.

Ordering products online can be frustrating. Sometimes, the items take ages to arrive, and when they do, they might not look like what you expected. The sizes could be wrong, or the colour might not match the picture. Returning items is often a hassle too, and you might have to pay for shipping.

Volunteering is a wonderful way to help others and make a difference in your community. It allows you to learn new skills and meet new people. By giving your time, you can support those who need it and make the world a better place, even if it's just a little bit.
